title: dodonaea viscosa subsp. angustifolia
intro: Dodonaea viscosa subsp. angustifolia, also known as the narrow-leaved hop-bush, is a plant species native to Australia, commonly found in arid and semi-arid regions. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic. In terms of herbal preparations, Dodonaea viscosa subsp. angustifolia is typically used as a decoction, made by boiling the leaves and stems in water, or as a topical cream or ointment to apply directly to the skin.
